description: Progress tracker handouts per lesson. Action items for students to complete and check off.

1-01-1.title: Supplier v Service Provider v Consultant
1-01-1.subtitle: Phase 1: Setup
actionItems: Identified my primary business model (Supplier / Service Provider / Consultant)
Reviewed implications for federal contracting
Documented my core value proposition
Decided on business structure for SAM.gov registration
1-01-1.notesPrompt: What structure fits my business best and why?

1-02-1.title: DUNS and UEI
1-02-1.subtitle: Phase 1: Setup
actionItems: Requested UEI (or confirmed existing UEI)
Verified UEI is linked to correct legal entity
Added UEI to business documents and email signature
1-02-1.notesPrompt: UEI number and where I'll use it

1-03-1.title: Creating Professional Email
1-03-1.subtitle: Phase 1: Setup
actionItems: Set up professional email with business domain
Created email signature with company name and contact info
Retired or deprioritized personal email for business use

1-04-1.title: How to Identify NAICS
1-04-1.subtitle: Phase 1: Setup
actionItems: Searched NAICS codes for my business type
Identified 3–10 codes that represent work I can do
Checked size standards for each code
Documented primary and secondary NAICS codes
1-04-1.notesPrompt: My NAICS codes and size standards

1-05-1.title: How to Create a SAM.gov Profile
1-05-1.subtitle: Phase 1: Setup
actionItems: Gathered: legal name, EIN, banking info, NAICS codes
Completed SAM.gov registration (or started process)
Verified entity matches IRS records
Opted in to DSBS when prompted

1-06-1.title: DSBS
1-06-1.subtitle: Phase 1: Setup
actionItems: Confirmed DSBS opt-in in SAM.gov
Completed DSBS profile with capabilities
Added differentiators and past performance highlights
Reviewed profile as a buyer would see it

1-07-1.title: Benefits / What to Say to Apex Accelerator
1-07-1.subtitle: Phase 1: Setup
actionItems: Found my local Apex Accelerator
Scheduled first counseling session
Prepared: what I sell, NAICS codes, SAM status
Brought 15 Questions handout to meeting
1-07-1.notesPrompt: Apex contact name and next steps

1-08-1.title: Capability Statement / Differentiators
1-08-1.subtitle: Phase 1: Setup
actionItems: Drafted capability statement (1–2 pages)
Included: overview, competencies, differentiators, past performance
Listed what makes me different from competitors
Had someone review for clarity in 30 seconds

2-01-1.title: How to Find Bid Opportunities
2-01-1.subtitle: Phase 2: Bidding
actionItems: Set up SAM.gov Contract Opportunities search
Saved searches by NAICS, keyword, set-aside
Enabled email alerts for new opportunities
Scheduled weekly time to check opportunities
2-01-1.notesPrompt: Saved search names and alert schedule

2-02-1.title: Assemble Team Based on Opportunities
2-02-1.subtitle: Phase 2: Bidding
actionItems: Identified gaps for a target opportunity
Listed potential partners or subcontractors
Contacted at least one potential team member
Reviewed teaming agreement template
2-02-1.notesPrompt: Team members and roles for next bid

2-03-1.title: Apply for Vendor/Supplier Credit
2-03-1.subtitle: Phase 2: Bidding
actionItems: Reviewed business credit options with bank
Applied for business line of credit or equivalent
Documented credit status for proposals
Added vendor credit to BD Progress Task List

2-04-1.title: Respond to Opportunity
2-04-1.subtitle: Phase 2: Bidding
actionItems: Read full solicitation and compliance matrix
Completed compliance matrix for this opportunity
Drafted technical approach and past performance
Prepared price proposal using estimating templates
Submitted bid before deadline
2-04-1.notesPrompt: Solicitation number, due date, lessons learned

2-05-1.title: Evaluate Bid Results
2-05-1.subtitle: Phase 2: Bidding
actionItems: Requested debrief (if lost)
Documented feedback from agency
Updated proposal templates based on feedback
Tracked what worked and what didn't

3-01-1.title: Identify Top 25 Buyers
3-01-1.subtitle: Phase 3: Business Development
actionItems: Used FPDS to find agencies buying in my NAICS
Built target list of top 25 agencies or buyers
Identified small business liaisons for each
Added contacts to CRM or tracking list

3-02-1.title: Setup and Attend Meetings with Government Buyers
3-02-1.subtitle: Phase 3: Business Development
actionItems: Scheduled meeting with at least one target buyer
Prepared using Capabilities Briefing Checklist
Brought capability statement and business cards
Followed up within 48 hours

3-03-1.title: Attend Industry Events
3-03-1.subtitle: Phase 3: Business Development
actionItems: Identified 2–3 upcoming industry events
Registered for at least one event
Prepared capability statements for distribution
Followed up with contacts within 48 hours

3-04-1.title: Attend Site Visits
3-04-1.subtitle: Phase 3: Business Development
actionItems: Identified site visit or industry day for target agency
Registered and attended
Met key personnel and documented names
Sent thank-you and follow-up within 48 hours
3-04-1.notesPrompt: Site visit date, facility, key contacts

3-05-1.title: Get on Supplier List for Top 25 Federal Suppliers
3-05-1.subtitle: Phase 3: Business Development
actionItems: Identified top 25 federal primes in my space
Registered in at least 3 prime vendor portals
Completed supplier diversity forms
Updated Prime Contractor Checklist
3-05-1.notesPrompt: Primes registered with and portal links

3-06-1.title: Monitor Contract Awards and Sub Opportunities
3-06-1.subtitle: Phase 3: Business Development
actionItems: Searched FPDS for recent awards in my NAICS
Identified primes who may need subcontractors
Reached out to at least one prime
Tracked IDIQs coming up for recompete

4-01-1.title: Apply for Small Business Certification
4-01-1.subtitle: Phase 4: Business Enhancement
actionItems: Confirmed eligibility using Size Standards Table
Chose certification(s) that fit (WOSB, SDVOSB, HUBZone, etc.)
Gathered required documents
Started or submitted application

4-02-1.title: 8(a) Certification
4-02-1.subtitle: Phase 4: Business Enhancement
actionItems: Confirmed 8(a) eligibility
Gathered required documentation
Started 8(a) application
Scheduled check-in with SBA or counselor

4-03-1.title: Mentor Protégé Program
4-03-1.subtitle: Phase 4: Business Enhancement
actionItems: Researched Mentor-Protégé requirements
Identified potential mentor firms
Reached out to at least one mentor
Reviewed joint venture and MPP structures

4-04-1.title: Self Performance Differentiator
4-04-1.subtitle: Phase 4: Business Enhancement
actionItems: Documented past performance for each project
Requested reference letters from clients
Updated capability statement with metrics
Drafted past performance narrative for proposals
4-04-1.notesPrompt: Top 3 past performance projects to highlight

4-05-1.title: Find Better Partners
4-05-1.subtitle: Phase 4: Business Enhancement
actionItems: Assessed current teaming partners
Identified gaps in past performance or capacity
Researched new potential partners
Initiated contact with at least one new partner

4-06-1.title: Identify Mid Size Mentor
4-06-1.subtitle: Phase 4: Business Enhancement
actionItems: Identified successful mid-size contractors in my space
Researched their past performance and contracts
Reached out to explore mentorship or teaming
Documented relationship and next steps
4-06-1.notesPrompt: Mid-size mentors identified and contact status

4-07-1.title: Speak at an Event
4-07-1.subtitle: Phase 4: Business Enhancement
actionItems: Identified local or industry event to speak at
Submitted speaker proposal or volunteered
Prepared presentation using sample deck
Promoted appearance on LinkedIn or email

5-01-1.title: System Registrations (PIEE, WAWF)
5-01-1.subtitle: Phase 5: Contract Management
actionItems: Confirmed contract requires PIEE/WAWF (if DoD)
Started PIEE registration with CO guidance
Registered in WAWF for invoicing
Verified access before first invoice

5-02-1.title: Subcontractor Compliance
5-02-1.subtitle: Phase 5: Contract Management
actionItems: Reviewed 10 Subcontractor Clauses handout
Flowed down required clauses to subs (if prime)
Verified subs meet small business requirements
Documented compliance for reporting

5-03-1.title: Project Compliance
5-03-1.subtitle: Phase 5: Contract Management
actionItems: Created deliverable tracking system
Documented all requirements and due dates
Submitted deliverables on time
Kept records of all submissions

5-04-1.title: Communication
5-04-1.subtitle: Phase 5: Contract Management
actionItems: Confirmed CO and PM contact info
Responded to all requests within 24–48 hours
Documented all communication
Reported issues early (no surprises)
